# Domain Rules

<!-- Generated by /ai-context. Manual edits are overwritten on re-run. -->

## AS IS — Current state

### Overview

- Digital certificate loaded from PFX file (real) or created in memory (simulated) — never persisted to disk.
- Domain rules are an in-memory list of allow/block entries tied to the current certificate.
- Proxy intercepts all HTTPS traffic on port 8888 and decides per-request whether to pass through or return a block page.
- Block-first evaluation: if any blocked rule matches, the request is rejected immediately.

### Certificate lifecycle

| Action | Behavior | Source |
|--------|----------|--------|
| **Load from PFX** | Read file bytes → construct `X509Certificate2` with `EphemeralKeySet` flag → dispose any previous cert → populate `CertificateInfo` with hardcoded allowed/blocked domains → rebuild domain rules → emit `StatusChanged` with subject, thumbprint, expiry, byte size | `CertBrokerService.LoadFromPfxBytes` (`Services/CertBrokerService.cs:35-79`) |
| **Load simulated** | Set `_certificate = null` (no real cert) → create `CertificateInfo` with `Thumbprint = "SIM-{guid}"` → same allowed/blocked domain lists → rebuild rules → emit `StatusChanged` | `CertBrokerService.LoadSimulatedCertificate` (`Services/CertBrokerService.cs:81-105`) |
| **Unload** | Dispose `X509Certificate2` → set `_certificate = null` → mark `CertificateInfo.IsActive = false` → clear domain rules → emit `StatusChanged` | `CertBrokerService.Unload` (`Services/CertBrokerService.cs:154-161`) |

### Domain rule evaluation (decision matrix)

`CertBrokerService.IsDomainAllowed(string domain)` — called from `ProxyService.OnBeforeRequest` (`Services/ProxyService.cs:79-96`):

| Input condition | Result | Side effect |
|-----------------|--------|-------------|
| Domain matches any `IsBlocked=true` rule (case-insensitive substring via `Contains`) | `false` (blocked) | Fires `DomainBlocked` event with the matching rule |
| Domain rule list is empty | `true` (allowed) | None |
| Domain matches any `IsBlocked=false` rule (case-insensitive substring via `Contains`) | `true` (allowed) | None |
| Domain matches no rule at all | `false` (blocked) | Fires `DomainBlocked` event |

Evaluation uses `ToLower().Trim()` normalization on the input domain and `ToLower()` on rule domains. Matching is **substring** — `"google.com"` matches `"www.google.com"`, `"mail.google.com"`, etc.

### Hardcoded default domains

On any certificate load (real or simulated), these domains are seeded:

| Allowed (✅) | Blocked (❌) |
|-------------|-------------|
| `download.dfe.sefin.ro.gov.br` | `google.com` |
| `sistemas.receita.fazenda.gov.br` | `facebook.com` |
| `portal.esocial.gov.br` | |
| `sped.fazenda.gov.br` | |

### Domain rule management

| Operation | Method | Behavior |
|-----------|--------|----------|
| Add or update | `AddDomainRule(domain, label, isBlocked)` | Removes existing rule for that domain (case-insensitive), adds new entry |
| Remove | `RemoveDomainRule(domain)` | Removes all rules matching the domain (case-insensitive) |
| Rebuild | `RebuildDomainRules()` | Clears all rules, rebuilds from `CertificateInfo.AllowedDomains` + `BlockedDomains` |

### Block page response

When a domain is blocked, `ProxyService.GenerateBlockedPage(domain)` (`Services/ProxyService.cs:98-119`) returns a self-contained HTML page with inline CSS:
- Dark background (`#1a1a2e`), red accent (`#e94560`)
- Card layout: "Acesso Bloqueado" heading, blocked domain name displayed in monospace, "CertGuard Mini esta protegendo seu trafego" message
- Response sent via `e.Ok(bytes)` — returns HTTP 200 with the block page body

### Certificate selection

When a request passes domain checks, `ProxyService.OnCertificateSelection` (`Services/ProxyService.cs:68-77`) injects `_broker.Certificate` (the `X509Certificate2`) as the client certificate. If `_broker.Certificate` is null (simulated mode), no client certificate is sent — the request passes through without certificate injection.
